skill.name: minimal-run-and-audit
skill.description: Trusted-lane execution and reporting skill for README-first AI repo reproduction with standardized output generation.
skill.summary: Executes smoke tests, inference runs, or evaluation commands and writes standardized reproduction outputs to repro_outputs/ directory.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 16 patterns, but 15 are false positives from misidentified file types (markdown docs flagged as Ruby, YAML config flagged as crypto). One true positive: Python subprocess.run for command execution is legitimate and properly sandboxed with timeout handling and shlex parsing. External command execution is the intended function of this skill.

low_findings.title: External Command Execution
low_findings.description: Script executes user-provided commands via subprocess.run with shlex.split parsing. Commands run with timeout protection (default 60s) and capture stdout/stderr. Risk is mitigated by timeout limits and lack of shell=True flag.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: subprocess.run is called with split_command output (shlex.split), no shell=True, and timeout enforcement - legitimate command execution pattern

content.user_title: Execute and audit AI repository reproduction commands
content.value_statement: Running AI paper reproduction experiments requires consistent command execution and standardized reporting. This skill executes smoke tests, inference runs, or evaluation commands while automatically generating structured output bundles for audit trails.
actual_capabilities: Execute user-specified commands with timeout protection and capture execution evidence
Generate standardized output bundles including SUMMARY.md, COMMANDS.md, LOG.md, and status.json
Track repository state changes via git status diff before and after command execution
Parse metrics from command output and identify best-performing results
Create PATCHES.md when repository files are modified during execution
Report clear execution status: success, partial, or blocked with actionable details
limitations: Does not execute training jobs or manage long-running training state
Does not perform initial repository setup or environment configuration
Does not select reproduction targets autonomously - requires user-specified commands
Command timeout defaults to 60 seconds, suitable for smoke tests but not full evaluations

use_cases.title: Smoke Test Execution
use_cases.description: Quickly verify a reproduction repository is functional by running documented smoke commands and generating standardized evidence bundles.

use_cases.title: Evaluation Run Normalization
use_cases.description: Execute evaluation commands from a paper's README and produce consistent output format for comparison across multiple reproduction attempts.

use_cases.title: Patch-Aware Execution Audit
use_cases.description: Run inference commands that may modify repository files, automatically tracking changes and generating patch documentation for audit trails.
use_cases.target_user: Open source maintainers tracking AI-assisted modifications

prompt_templates.title: Basic Smoke Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the smoke test command from the README and generate standardized outputs in repro_outputs/
prompt_templates.scenario: You need to quickly verify a repository is functional before deeper reproduction work.

prompt_templates.title: Inference Execution with Metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute the inference command specified in docs/evaluation.md with 120 second timeout and capture all metrics from stdout.
prompt_templates.scenario: Running a documented evaluation command and need structured metric extraction.

prompt_templates.title: Git-Aware Command with Patch Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the fine-tuning verification command and generate PATCHES.md documenting any repository modifications made during execution.
prompt_templates.scenario: Command may modify config files or generate checkpoints that should be tracked.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Command Audit Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute these three verification commands in sequence: sanity check, inference test, and metric validation. Generate a consolidated SUMMARY.md with pass/fail status for each command.
prompt_templates.scenario: Running a multi-stage verification pipeline and need unified reporting.

output_examples.input: Smoke test command that completes successfully
output_examples.output: status.json reports success status, SUMMARY.md shows command completed with zero exit code, LOG.md contains captured stdout/stderr, COMMANDS.md lists the executed command for reproducibility

output_examples.input: Inference command that times out after 60 seconds
output_examples.output: status.json reports partial status with timeout indicator, SUMMARY.md shows command ran for full timeout period, execution log contains partial output before timeout

output_examples.input: Evaluation command that modifies configuration files
output_examples.output: status.json includes changed_files list, PATCHES.md documents git diff of modifications, SUMMARY.md notes patch state with reference to full audit

best_practices: Always specify explicit timeout values appropriate for your command type - smoke tests need 30-60s, inference may need 120-300s
Review generated PATCHES.md before committing any repository modifications made during execution
Use git status verification to understand what files changed during command execution
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for training execution - it is designed for short non-training commands only
Do not run untrusted commands without reviewing them first - while sandboxed, commands still execute with your user permissions
Do not ignore partial status reports - timeout or non-zero exit codes indicate the command did not complete as expected

faq.question: What types of commands should I run with this skill?
faq.answer: Use this for smoke tests, inference runs, evaluation commands, and sanity checks that complete within minutes. Do not use for training jobs or long-running processes.

faq.question: How does the skill handle command timeouts?
faq.answer: Commands timeout after the specified duration (default 60 seconds). The skill captures partial output and reports status as partial with timeout details in the execution log.

faq.question: What files does the skill generate?
faq.answer: Standard outputs go to repro_outputs/ directory: SUMMARY.md for human-readable results, COMMANDS.md for reproducibility, LOG.md for full output, and status.json for machine-readable state.

faq.question: Does this skill work with non-Git repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, but git-based change tracking will be unavailable. The skill detects if a directory is a git worktree and adjusts evidence collection accordingly.

faq.question: How are metrics extracted from command output?
faq.answer: The skill parses key=value or key:number patterns from stdout/stderr, filters out noise like loss/lr/time/mem, and identifies the best metric for summary reporting.

faq.question: What happens if my command modifies repository files?
faq.answer: The skill detects file changes via git status diff and generates PATCHES.md documenting modifications. This enables audit trails for AI-assisted code changes.
